concepts.title: Singular vs smooth points
concepts.blurb: A point $p$ on a variety $X = V(f_1,\ldots,f_r) \subset \mathbb{A}^n$ is smooth iff the Jacobian $(\partial f_i / \partial x_j)(p)$ has the expected rank $n - \dim X$. The tangent space $T_pX = \ker J(p)$ jumps in dimension precisely at singular points. The tangent cone $C_pX$ — the lowest-degree term of $f_i$ at $p$ — refines this: at a smooth point $T_pX = C_pX$, but at a node both lines of the cone live inside the bigger tangent space.

concepts.title: Blowing up a subvariety
concepts.blurb: The blow-up $\mathrm{Bl}_Z X \to X$ replaces a closed subvariety $Z \subset X$ by its projectivized normal directions: $\mathrm{Bl}_p \mathbb{A}^n = \{(x, \ell) : x \in \ell\} \subset \mathbb{A}^n \times \mathbb{P}^{n-1}$. It is a proper birational morphism, an iso away from $Z$, and is universal: any map to $X$ along which the ideal of $Z$ becomes locally principal factors uniquely through $\mathrm{Bl}_Z X$. Each blow-up tries to convert a singularity into a milder one — possibly smooth.

concepts.title: Hironaka's theorem
concepts.blurb: For any variety $X$ over a characteristic-zero field, there exists a proper birational morphism $\pi : \tilde X \to X$ with $\tilde X$ smooth and $\pi$ an isomorphism over the smooth locus of $X$. Hironaka (1964) proved this by an inductive sequence of blow-ups along smooth centres lying inside the singular locus, with an invariant that strictly decreases. In characteristic $p$ the analogous statement is open in dimension $\ge 4$.

concepts.title: Curves: nodes, cusps, normalisation
concepts.blurb: For a curve, resolution is classical and one-step: the normalisation $\tilde C \to C$ replaces $C$'s coordinate ring by its integral closure in the function field, separating branches at every singular point. A node $y^2 = x^2$ blows up to two disjoint smooth points; a cusp $y^2 = x^3$ to one smooth point with a Newton–Puiseux parametrisation $t \mapsto (t^2, t^3)$. Normalisation, blow-up at the singularity, and Puiseux series all give the same answer in dimension one.

concepts.title: Surfaces and ADE
concepts.blurb: For surfaces, the simplest singularities are the rational double points (Du Val, ADE): $A_n: x^2 + y^2 + z^{n+1}$, $D_n$, $E_6, E_7, E_8$. Their minimal resolutions have exceptional divisor a tree of $\mathbb{P}^1$'s whose intersection graph is the Dynkin diagram of the same name. Brieskorn realised them as quotients $\mathbb{C}^2 / \Gamma$ for finite subgroups $\Gamma \subset \mathrm{SL}_2(\mathbb{C})$; the McKay correspondence matches irreducible $\Gamma$-representations with exceptional curves.

concepts.title: Applications and frontiers
concepts.blurb: Resolution is the entry point to the minimal model program (Mori): contract redundant exceptional curves to find a canonical birational model. Log resolutions — making both $X$ and a divisor simple normal crossing — power motivic integration (Kontsevich) and weak factorisation. Characteristic-$p$ resolution, de Jong's alterations, and resolution of foliations remain active frontiers.
